articles.title: BTS’ New Album ‘Arirang’ Is Finally Here - Rolling Stone
articles.description: BTS have released their fifth studio album, 'Arirang,' which you can stream now.

articles.title: 'Mormon Wives' Jessi Draper Ngatikaura Husband Files For Divorce - TMZ
articles.description: More drama for "The Secret Lives of Mormon" wives ... Jessi Draper Ngatikuara's husband just filed for divorce ... TMZ has learned.

articles.title: Bachelorette's new season pulled after Taylor Frankie Paul abuse allegations - BBC
articles.description: The decision comes after footage emerged appearing to show the reality star in an altercation with her ex-partner.

articles.title: ‘Project Hail Mary’ To Orbit Around $100M+ WW Opening; ‘Ready Or Not 2’ $14M WW – Preview - Deadline
articles.description: This weekend the Amazon MGM Studios Ryan Gosling movie 'Project Hail Mary' is seeing a $100M global start, while 'Ready or Not 2' is eyeing $14M worldwide.

articles.title: Chanel Tops the Oscars Red Carpet Power Rankings, Thanks to Teyana Taylor, Jessie Buckley and Nicole Kidman - The Hollywood Reporter
articles.description: TV ratings for the ceremony itself were unusually low, but interest in the fashion — as measured by social media engagement — has never been higher.

articles.title: Afroman prevails in cops' music video defamation suit after a brief but viral trial - NPR
articles.description: The "Because I Got High" rapper made waves in 2023 with the album and song "Lemon Pound Cake," using home video to mock a police raid on his Ohio home. The deputies lost their civil suit against him.

articles.title: 'Project Hail Mary' Writer on Ryan Gosling, Making 'The Matrix 5' - Variety
articles.description: Drew Goddard talks about adapting 'Project Hail Mary,' directing 'The Matrix 5' and his love of 'Weapons' and 'Sinners.'

articles.title: David Ellison Sends Letter to CA Lawmakers Outlining Plan to Keep Hollywood Jobs Local (Exclusive) - The Hollywood Reporter
articles.description: In a letter to Sen. Adam Schiff and Rep. Laura Friedman, the Paramount and soon-to-be Warner Bros. studio chief argued that keeping key aspects of the historic studios separate will help workers and L.A.
